title: micrandropsis scleroxylon
intro: Micrandropsis scleroxylon, also known as Tiama or Iroko, is a tropical tree species native to West and Central Africa. The medicinal properties of Micrandropsis scleroxylon lie in its bark, which has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of Micrandropsis scleroxylon include teas, infusions, and ointments made from its bark, which are traditionally used in African folk medicine.
